_comment: Case-tolerant SI prefixes for Atlas unit strings. SHARED by
lib/services/atlasMapper.ts (imports it) and scripts/atlas-ingest.mjs
(readFileSync's it) so the live ingest path and the TS copy cannot drift.
Same pattern as atlas-gaia-dicts.json.
WHY: applyUnitPrefix tested unit.startsWith('p') — lowercase only. Suppliers
write capitals, so '4010 PF' stored 4010 instead of 4.01e-9 while '6.5 mΩ' on
the SAME product converted fine (reproduced on data/atlas/mfr_389_AK_奥科_params.json,
model AK4080).
WHY THIS SHAPE AND NOT CASE-INSENSITIVE MATCHING: measured over all 429 source
files, folding the first letter would newly 'convert' 14,840 values reading
'Pin' (pin count), 39,645 reading 'P', 7,106 reading 'N' and 342 reading 'Nm'
(newton-metre). Only ~2,100 of ~29,600 case-mismatched tokens are genuine
prefixes. The lowercase-only rule was PROTECTING the rest.
So a prefix applies only when the REMAINDER is exactly one of the atoms below,
in canonical case. Verified over all 429 files: converts 2,086 values across
PF/UA/PA/Ps/Us and leaves 661,728 untouched.
⚠️ Do NOT reuse UNIT_ATOMS from mappingHealthCore.ts here. That list is
deliberately broad for classification and contains ['m', 'length'], which would
make 'Nm' split into nano+metre and silently convert 342 newton-metre values.
A converter needs a narrow list; a classifier needs a wide one.

_prefixNotes.P: pico. 'P' is peta in strict SI, but peta does not occur in component specs; PF=picofarad, PA=picoamp do.
_prefixNotes.U: micro — the ASCII stand-in for µ that suppliers type.
_prefixNotes.N: DELIBERATELY ABSENT. 'Nm' (newton-metre, 342x) outnumbers 'Ns' (nanosecond, 4x) ~85:1, and no lexical rule separates them. Refusing N loses 8 values and protects 342.
_prefixNotes.M: DELIBERATELY ABSENT. 'M' already means mega in the canonical path — 'MΩ' (9,038x) and 'MHz' (14,681x) are correct today and must not be re-read as milli.
_prefixNotes.F/A/G/T/K: DELIBERATELY ABSENT. F is farad, A is amp, and G/T/K are already correct uppercase prefixes.
